SEO Insertions on What is Group Superannuation Scheme and its Advantages for Employees? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: group superannuation scheme, group insurance policy for employees, group life insurance
Body Content:
It’s never too early to start making plans to secure one’s retirement years. While there are several ways of initiating this process, a group superannuation scheme is definitely one of the most prudent options to consider.
While such group insurance policies for employees are pretty common in India, a significant proportion of the workforce seems to be unaware of what such plans entail, let alone be aware of the fund reserves they stand to derive at retirement. This makes understanding such schemes and their benefits for employee’s mandatory.
Understanding a Group Superannuation Scheme
Group superannuation plans are a type of group insurance policy for employees that extend retirement benefits to them once their service tenure ends.  Under such schemes, the employer contributes a certain percentage (up to 15%) of the employee’s pay towards this retirement benefit fund1.
In other words, group superannuation schemes are designed for employers to help their employees manage their pension funds. While most companies tend to buy such insurance products directly from well-known insurers, some manage them through their own trusts or opt for a superannuation benefit fund from a renowned insurance company. The payout from such plans help employees retain their financial independence even during their retirement years.
Advantages of Group Superannuation Insurance Plans
Group life insurance plans with superannuation benefits are rewarding for both the employer and the employee. However, the scale of benefits tips in favour of the latter rather than the former. Some of the benefits of such plans for employees include:
After Retirement Stability- Employees can withdraw up to one-third of the accumulated benefit at the time of retirement and convert the rest into a regular pension. This steady flow of income helps them live a comfortable and worry-free life after retirement. Moreover, if the selected policy offers a guaranteed investment return for every policy year, policyholders can enjoy a sizable corpus during retirement.
Tax-Proof Benefits: Under Section 80C of the Indian Income Tax Act, any contributions employees make towards the superannuation fund are eligible for tax deductions of up to ₹ 1.5 lakhs. Additionally, both the interest and death benefits earned from a superannuation scheme are completely tax-free.
Death Benefits: Certain group life insurance plans with superannuation benefits offer instant death benefits to those covered by the plan. This helps the employee’s family manage expenses after the individual’s untimely demise.
FAQs Related to Group Superannuation Insurance Plans
1.  What is a group superannuation scheme?
A group superannuation scheme is essentially a retirement benefit or pension fund that’s managed by your employer. By saving a part of your pay during your employment tenure, your employer ensures that you continue enjoying a steady source of income after retirement. The amount accumulated in the fund is paid to you at retirement.
2. Can I transfer my superannuation scheme after I change my job?
Yes. You can easily transfer the fund to your new employer’s superannuation scheme. If your new employer doesn’t have a superannuation scheme in place, you can either withdraw the accumulated amount or keep it in the fund until retirement.
3. What documents do I need when filing a death claim under a superannuation plan?
To file a death claim under such a plan, you need the following documents:
A carefully filed and attested copy of the claim form provided by the insurance company
The original policy documents
The insured individual’s death certificate
For direct transfers, a cancelled check or bank passbook that proves the name and identity of the claimant
A valid photo ID
In case of accidental deaths, you may also need to furnish a police report along with these documents. Once the claim is processed, the amount will be paid to the listed nominee of the insured individual.
4. What are the eligibility criteria for a group superannuation scheme?
The eligibility criteria for a group insurance plan with superannuation benefits are as follows:
Minimum entry age- 18 years
Maximum entry age- 75 years
Minimum group size- 10 members
Maximum group size- None
5. What are the types of annuity options under group superannuation plans?
The following annuity payment options are available to group superannuation policyholders:
Annuity payments for life
Annuity payments for life guaranteed for 5, 10 or 15 years
Annuity payments for life plus a return on capital
Annuity payments on both husband and wife’s life
6. What are the tax benefits under a group superannuation plan?
An employer’s contribution to the scheme is classed as a tax-deductible by the Income Tax Department. Any contribution made by the employee towards this scheme is eligible for a tax exemption of up to ₹ 1.5 lakhs under Section 80C of the Indian Income Tax Act, 1961. However, the general income tax slab rates are applicable on the pension earned through such group superannuation schemes.
Summing It Up
Investing in your retirement years is both prudent and necessary. A group superannuation scheme helps secure your retirement years, allowing you to take care of your retirement goals without falling behind on your other commitments. In the present, contributions to this a group superannuation scheme gives you tax benefits, while in the future the regular income from the sizable retirement corpus built over time helps cover food and rent expenses, take care of medical bills, and tackle any other unforeseen problems that may arise post-retirement.
